briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for July 22, 2026. Today, we will focus on various aspects of the intensifying global climate crisis and the domestic and international efforts to address it.

sections.title: 🔴 The Reality of Climate Catastrophes Striking the World
sections.content: Domestically today, extreme weather patterns were simultaneously observed, with heavy rainfall of up to 80mm in the Seoul metropolitan area and Gangwon region, while scorching heatwaves prevailed in the southern regions [43]. Internationally, sudden floods in eastern Afghanistan resulted in dozens of deaths, injuries, and missing persons [73]. In Ontario, Canada, widespread damage was reported due to wildfires, with two First Nations communities completely burned down and evacuation orders issued for a total of 13 communities [72]. Canada's smoke-filled skies are raising public health concerns, and New York City also experienced smoke-laden air [56, 61]. Furthermore, scientists have warned that up to 13 million people in the UK could face long-term coastal flood risks due to the climate crisis, and a study also revealed that the likelihood of snow droughts in the Western U.S. has quadrupled due to climate change [71, 52]. These phenomena clearly demonstrate that climate change is no longer a future threat but a present reality.

sections.title: ⚠️ Socioeconomic Risks Exacerbated by Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change is causing widespread risks across society, extending beyond mere natural disasters. Analysis suggests that climate change will emerge as a core banking risk in 2026 [51], and domestically, concerns have been raised that the number of displaced persons has reached an all-time high due to the convergence of conflict and the climate crisis [11]. In the agricultural sector, as climate change-induced damage to agriculture increases, there are calls for strengthening proactive response systems and securing budgets [8, 15, 16]. Furthermore, climate change is altering the coffee map, enabling Thai Arabica coffee to challenge the premium market [1, 4], and is severely impacting ecosystems, with California's native wildflowers facing extinction as they fail to adapt to climate change [48]. News that Antarctic cruise guides are leaving their jobs due to climate change also illustrates its direct impact on specific industries [44]. Even the increasing number of tick bite cases in the U.S. and UK suggests that climate change poses new threats to public health [74]. Researchers are urging changes in crop development methods to address issues of hunger, malnutrition, and climate change [57]. Proactive and multifaceted responses to these complex risks are urgently needed.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Domestic Renewable Energy Transition and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, positive movements are active for the spread of renewable energy and the realization of carbon neutrality. Community participation is expanding as renewable energy facilities can now be installed in the Hoedong Reservoir area, allowing residents of water source protection zones to increase their income through the expansion of renewable energy projects [24, 32, 26]. Notably, a system to convert electricity generated by residential solar power facilities into revenue assets and issue and trade certificates will be introduced from August, encouraging general households to participate in renewable energy production [31, 33, 39, 37, 42]. The Ministry of Climate and the Korea Energy Agency are supporting corporate RE100 implementation through a pilot project for self-consumption renewable energy certification and expanding the renewable energy market by introducing a pilot REGO (Renewable Energy Guarantees of Origin) system for private solar power [25, 38, 40]. Concurrently, carbon neutrality efforts are continuing in various sectors, including Suwon City's carbon-neutral green city creation project, Chungnam's expansion of resource circulation villages and carbon neutrality cooperation with Jiangsu Province, and Seoul City's promotion of carbon neutrality for delivery containers [19, 20, 21, 22]. Lotte E&C is also gaining attention for its responsible corporate actions, such as publishing a sustainability report incorporating global ESG disclosure standards and precisely analyzing climate and natural risks [14, 17]. Meanwhile, there has been a call for transparency, arguing that when sharing renewable energy profits, information should be disclosed to residents based on 'actual expected income,' increasing demands for transparency [34, 36].

sections.title: 🇰🇷🌍 Strengthening Resilience Through Climate Crisis Adaptation and Expanded Citizen Participation
sections.content: Citizen participation and educational programs for climate crisis adaptation and strengthening resilience are also actively underway. Yongin Special City is organizing various experiential programs for children and citizens during the summer vacation, such as 'The Most Exciting Summer to Save the Earth: Climate Crisis Adaptation Operation,' to raise awareness about the importance of responding to the climate crisis [2, 6, 7, 10, 13]. The Wonju Climate Change Promotion Center is also operating summer vacation carbon neutrality experiential education to enhance environmental awareness among future generations [3]. Incheon City and Jung-gu are also encouraging community participation by holding policy discussions and resolution rallies to expand citizen-led carbon neutrality practices [18, 23]. Internationally, Snohomish County regional leaders emphasized cooperation as key to solving climate change [49], and news has also emerged that summer camps are exploring ways to adapt to climate change [50]. Israel has launched a $2 million marine renewable energy program to develop climate adaptation technologies [35], and an EU urban regeneration experiment in Rome, where 'forgotten peripheries' are being revitalized through climate adaptation, demonstrates the potential for building climate-resilient cities [9]. The Incheon Institute is strengthening cooperation with DHI on water management and climate response, continuing adaptation efforts through a scientific approach [5].

briefing.closing: As we have reviewed in today's briefing, climate change is emerging as a global threat, necessitating urgent responses, accelerated transition to renewable energy, and intensified adaptation efforts. We hope to see continued expansion of various initiatives and citizen participation both domestically and internationally. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: Millions face long-term coastal flooding risk in UK without climate action, scientists warn
articles.description: A ‘reasonable worst-case scenario’ suggests need for large-scale movement of populations away from reshaped coastUp to 13 million people in the UK face the long-term risk of coastal flooding due to the climate crisis, scientists have warned, with a “reasonable worst-case scenario” suggesting the need for the large-scale movement of populations away from a dramatically reshaped coast.Even if today’s global pledges to cut carbon pollution are met, about 1.4 million more people will be exposed to c

summary.phenomenon: Scientists have warned that up to 13 million people in the UK face the long-term risk of coastal flooding due to the climate crisis. Even if current pledges to cut carbon pollution are met, approximately 1.4 million more people will be exposed to this risk.
summary.cause: This risk arises due to the climate crisis.
summary.outlook: According to a reasonable worst-case scenario, large-scale population movement away from dramatically reshaped coastal areas may be necessary.

articles.description: Chiefs in Ontario say they were left to self-evacuate before homes burned to ground, after being told there was no imminent threatFast-moving wildfires have burned two First Nations communities in Canada to the ground, as members said they were left to self-evacuate without government help or emergency alerts.Thirteen communities in Ontario province have been ordered to evacuate because of wildfires, most of them First Nations. summary.phenomenon: Fast-moving wildfires in Ontario, Canada, have burned two First Nations communities to the ground, and a total of 13 communities have been ordered to evacuate. Leaders of these communities stated that they had to self-evacuate without government assistance or emergency alerts.

articles.description: Experts share tips to avoid the blood-sucking pests that can transmit Lyme disease and other illnesses on to humansI won’t go into gory detail about the tick I found latched behind my ear approximately 35 hours after a hike in early June. Suffice it to say, the parasite was well fed, and I was horrified.I am far from alone in making such a disgusting discovery. summary.phenomenon: Tick bite cases are increasing in the United States and the United Kingdom, with unusually high emergency room visits for tick bites reported by the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
